name: WHO-5 Well-Being Index
purpose: Subjective well-being over the past 2 weeks. Short, sensitive to change, validated in many languages.
citation: Topp, C. W., Østergaard, S. D., Søndergaard, S., & Bech, P. (2015). The WHO-5 Well-Being Index: a systematic review of the literature. Psychotherapy and Psychosomatics, 84(3), 167-176.
license: Free to use, copy, translate, distribute. Originally released by the WHO Regional Office for Europe (1998).

options.label: More than half of the time
Less than half of the time

stem: Please indicate for each of the five statements which is closest to how you have been feeling over the last two weeks. Notice that higher numbers mean better well-being.

items.prompt: I have felt cheerful and in good spirits
I have felt calm and relaxed
I have felt active and vigorous
I woke up feeling fresh and rested
My daily life has been filled with things that interest me

flag_rules.notes: Score ≤28 is the validated threshold for further depression screening per the WHO-5 manual.
